source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The <image> shows an inverter circuit with a DC source voltage \( V_s \). The semiconductor switches of the inverter are operated such that the pole voltages \( V_{10} \) and \( V_{20} \) are as shown in the <image>. What is the RMS value of the pole-to-pole voltage \( V_{12} \)?

multi_step: The task likely requires combining two visual pole-voltage waveforms to form V12 = V10 - V20

This is more than direct lookup because it involves waveform interpretation plus electrical/mathematical calculation
